tossups.question: The Birmingham Group learned this technique from a handbook translated by Edwardian artist Christiana Herringham. Paul Cadmus and Jacob Lawrence contributed to a 20th-century revival of this technique, which Andrew Wyeth learned from Peter Hurd and used for Christina’s World. Cennino Cennini’s Craftsman’s Handbook provided ratios for this medium, which was used with encaustic for the Fayum mummy portraits. A plaster and rabbit-skin glue mixture called gesso (“JESS-soh”) was first developed as a ground for this medium, which may use casein (“KAY-seen”) glue. Medieval panel painting like the gold-leaf-adorned work of Duccio was painted in this medium, which was fully supplanted by oil painting during the Renaissance. For 10 points, what medium mixes pigments into binders such as egg yolk?

packet.name: L – Columbia A, Liberty A, Oxford A, Oxford C, Purdue A, Toronto A

tossups.question_sanitized: The Birmingham Group learned this technique from a handbook translated by Edwardian artist Christiana Herringham. Paul Cadmus and Jacob Lawrence contributed to a 20th-century revival of this technique, which Andrew Wyeth learned from Peter Hurd and used for Christina's World. Cennino Cennini's Craftsman's Handbook provided ratios for this medium, which was used with encaustic for the Fayum mummy portraits. A plaster and rabbit-skin glue mixture called gesso ("JESS-soh") was first developed as a ground for this medium, which may use casein ("KAY-seen") glue. Medieval panel painting like the gold-leaf-adorned work of Duccio was painted in this medium, which was fully supplanted by oil painting during the Renaissance. For 10 points, what medium mixes pigments into binders such as egg yolk?

bonuses.leadin: Older examples of these paintings were created on hollowed-out wooden boards fastened together by back slats. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Older examples of these paintings were created on hollowed-out wooden boards fastened together by back slats. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these paintings whose “material frame” is defined by the area of their recessed ark. Some of these paintings are protected by a decorative gold or silver cover called a riza (“ree-zah”).
Like other panel works at the time, most medieval Russian icons were painted using this medium. Before being supplanted by oil painting, this was the most popular painting medium in Medieval Europe.
Instead of wooden boards, artists from Palekh painted their icon miniatures onto this material. This material is more known for its use in piñata creation.
parts_sanitized: Name these paintings whose "material frame" is defined by the area of their recessed ark. Some of these paintings are protected by a decorative gold or silver cover called a riza ("ree-zah").
Like other panel works at the time, most medieval Russian icons were painted using this medium. Before being supplanted by oil painting, this was the most popular painting medium in Medieval Europe.
Instead of wooden boards, artists from Palekh painted their icon miniatures onto this material. This material is more known for its use in pinata creation.
answers: Russian icons [or Eastern Orthodox icons or Byzantine icons; prompt on religious paintings; prompt on miniature paintings]
papier-mâché [or paper mache; prompt on newspaper; prompt on cartons; prompt cardboard]
answers_sanitized: Russian icons [or Eastern Orthodox icons or Byzantine icons; prompt on religious paintings; prompt on miniature paintings]
papier-mache [or paper mache; prompt on newspaper; prompt on cartons; prompt cardboard]

bonuses.leadin: This painting, which was described by Henry James as the “saddest work of art in the world,” has a 15 minute time limit for viewing due to its condition. For 10 points each.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This painting, which was described by Henry James as the "saddest work of art in the world," has a 15 minute time limit for viewing due to its condition. For 10 points each.
parts: Name this Leonardo da Vinci painting that depicts a table with the twelve apostles and Jesus, whose feet are cut off.
A primary reason for the Last Supper’s deterioration is its innovative but unstable mixture of oil paints with this other type of paint. This medium often involves the use of egg yolks.

Less than 60 years after its painting, this artist described The Last Supper as a “muddle of blots” that was nearly unrecognizable. This artist wrote about many Renaissance artists such as da Vinci in his pioneering work Lives of the Great Artists.
parts_sanitized: Name this Leonardo da Vinci painting that depicts a table with the twelve apostles and Jesus, whose feet are cut off.
A primary reason for the Last Supper's deterioration is its innovative but unstable mixture of oil paints with this other type of paint. This medium often involves the use of egg yolks.

Less than 60 years after its painting, this artist described The Last Supper as a "muddle of blots" that was nearly unrecognizable. This artist wrote about many Renaissance artists such as da Vinci in his pioneering work Lives of the Great Artists.
answers_sanitized: The Last Supper [or Il Cenacolo or L'Ultima Cena]

bonuses.leadin: A white figure emerges from an orange tree in a painting in this medium titled The Kitchen Garden on the Eyot. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artistic medium. A giantess enshrouded in golden wheat is circled by geese in a 20th-century painting in this medium, which was also used for late medieval paintings, such as those by Duccio.

This artist used eggs to depict eggs in her tempera paintings The Giantess (The Guardian of the Egg) and The Kitchen Garden on the Eyot. A self-portrait by this British-born artist shows her sitting next to a lactating hyena.
Carrington wrote that the egg was “the dividing line between the Big and the Small” during her stay at a sanatorium during this conflict. This conflict also inspired Pablo Picasso’s Guernica.
answers_sanitized: Spanish Civil War [or Guerra Civil Espanola]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A white figure emerges from an orange tree in a painting in this medium titled The Kitchen Garden on the Eyot. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this artistic medium. A giantess enshrouded in golden wheat is circled by geese in a 20th-century painting in this medium, which was also used for late medieval paintings, such as those by Duccio.

This artist used eggs to depict eggs in her tempera paintings The Giantess (The Guardian of the Egg) and The Kitchen Garden on the Eyot. A self-portrait by this British-born artist shows her sitting next to a lactating hyena.
Carrington wrote that the egg was "the dividing line between the Big and the Small" during her stay at a sanatorium during this conflict. This conflict also inspired Pablo Picasso's Guernica.

bonuses.leadin: Early Renaissance panel paintings were prepared with animal fat and gypsum-based gesso before being painted with this type of paint. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this type of paint used by European painters before the invention of oil paints in the 15th century. This thin and matte-colored paint was made using eggs.

Tempera paints were used when painting fresco, which by definition involves painting on this material. Typically the arriccio type of this material was applied to a brick wall before the application of the finer intonaco type.
The Creation of Adam is a fresco painting by this artist, part of his Sistine Chapel ceiling.
answers: Michelangelo di Lodovico Buonarroti Simoni [accept either underlined portion]
answers_sanitized: Michelangelo di Lodovico Buonarroti Simoni [accept either underlined portion]

parts_sanitized: Name this type of paint used by European painters before the invention of oil paints in the 15th century. This thin and matte-colored paint was made using eggs.

Tempera paints were used when painting fresco, which by definition involves painting on this material. Typically the arriccio type of this material was applied to a brick wall before the application of the finer intonaco type.
The Creation of Adam is a fresco painting by this artist, part of his Sistine Chapel ceiling.

bonuses.leadin: Cennino Cennini’s Craftsman’s Handbook includes recipes for paints used in this medium. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these hand-painted books, first created in monasteries, that were decorated with gold or silver leaf.
Cennini’s Handbook is the first extensive document to discuss this paint medium, which uses egg yolk as a binder. This medium was largely replaced by oil painting.

The fact that Cennini’s Handbook mentions oil painting disproves Giorgio Vasari’s claim that oil painting was invented by this artist, who may have worked on an illuminated manuscript called the Turin-Milan Hours. He often signed his paintings with a Flemish motto meaning “As I can.”
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Cennino Cennini's Craftsman's Handbook includes recipes for paints used in this medium. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name these hand-painted books, first created in monasteries, that were decorated with gold or silver leaf.
Cennini's Handbook is the first extensive document to discuss this paint medium, which uses egg yolk as a binder. This medium was largely replaced by oil painting.

The fact that Cennini's Handbook mentions oil painting disproves Giorgio Vasari's claim that oil painting was invented by this artist, who may have worked on an illuminated manuscript called the Turin-Milan Hours. He often signed his paintings with a Flemish motto meaning "As I can."

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about visual art techniques, for 10 points each.
parts: In this paint medium, egg yolk is commonly used to bind the pigments. In order to prevent it from cracking, it must be applied in thin layers, which often creates a translucent appearance.
This country's art forms include "pictures of the floating world," or ukiyo-e prints, created by artists such as Katsushika Hokusai.

In this other printmaking technique, the image is inscribed into a surface and ink is poured into the resulting grooves, after which paper is pressed onto the surface from above to transfer the ink. Francisco Goya utilized this in creating his Caprichos and Disasters of War series.
answers: intaglio [also accept aquatint since all the clues apply to that as well]
answers_sanitized: intaglio [also accept aquatint since all the clues apply to that as well]

bonuses.leadin: Identify the following about some art terms from the Italian Renaissance, for 10 points each.
parts: This medium, described in detail in a work by Cennino Cennini, involves using egg yolk to bind pigments. It was largely supplanted by oil painting around 1500.
All lines converge into one to three vanishing points in the linear form of this technique, which provides the illusion of depth for a two-dimensional painting.
This term comes from the Italian word for “obscure”. It was frequently employed by Caravaggio and is characterized by works with a mainly dark tonality.

All lines converge into one to three vanishing points in the linear form of this technique, which provides the illusion of depth for a two-dimensional painting.
This term comes from the Italian word for "obscure". It was frequently employed by Caravaggio and is characterized by works with a mainly dark tonality.
